title: Fluent Agents Studio — the complete user guide, from zero to hero

options.reader: a friendly, patient product instructor — warm, encouraging, crystal clear, never condescending
options.audience: complete beginners to video and voice-over production who have never used a media tool before
options.slide_style: An instructional deck for complete beginners: friendly, uncluttered, step-by-step. Place the six screenshots on the slides where the narration walks through those exact screens — match each screenshot to its section (form, script page, slides page, review overlay, outputs page, templates page). Design simple teaching figures: the four-station pipeline as a labeled flow diagram, the review-revise-rerender loop as a cycle, and a which-output-do-I-need comparison. Use big_statement slides for the key principles (you describe, the studio produces

revisions.instructions: Merge lines 0, 1 and 2 into ONE short, warm welcome line that says the product name exactly once — e.g. introducing this as the complete beginner's guide to the studio, then flowing straight into the promise of the guide. Keep every other line unchanged.

The source document has been completely rewritten (it now describes every screen visually for someone who has never opened the app). Rewrite the ENTIRE script as a faithful readout of the new source, in the same warm instructor voice: one short welcome line naming the product once, then the full document, with headings becoming natural spoken transitions.

The source's 'Station four: the outputs' section gained new material: a YouTube card — one-time five-minute channel connection, then one-button publishing that uploads as private for review first, with the link saved on the project. Update ONLY the outputs line(s) of the script to faithfully include this

The source's 'Your first look at the studio' section gained a new opening paragraph: the studio's web address fluentagents.com greets you with a landing page — the front door — where this guide plays as a video with real example productions beneath it, and an amber Open the Studio button leads into the workbench where the tour begins. Insert this faithfully as narration at that point in the script. Keep every other line word-for-word unchanged, including the YouTube material in the outputs line.
